examples: Rather than offering a voucher when something goes awry, she suggests that businesses should hand over the real deal to assuage upset customers.
To assuage his wife's grief, he took her on a tour of Europe.
The booming economy seems to have done little to assuage this underlying generational sense of anxious urgency, especially for those who have college debt.
Relying on foreign resources may also assuage government concerns about having enough food, he said.
So, once again, the perpetrators of the crime are trying to assuage the victims.
